expectations.text: Response installs react-grab as dev dependency
expectations.evidence: Shows 'npm install -D react-grab' command. Explains 'Installing it under devDependencies makes the intent clear and prevents accidental inclusion in production dependency lists.'

expectations.text: Response uses import.meta.env.DEV for Vite dev-only check
expectations.evidence: Uses 'if (import.meta.env.DEV) { import("react-grab"); }' and explains 'Vite statically replaces import.meta.env.DEV at build time. In development it evaluates to true... In production builds the entire if block is eliminated via dead-code elimination.'

expectations.text: Response adds script in index.html before </head>
expectations.evidence: Explicitly states 'Open your index.html (at the project root) and add the following script block before the closing </head> tag' with a <script type="module"> block.

expectations.text: Response confirms react-grab is excluded from production builds
expectations.evidence: Section 6 'Verify production safety' instructs to run 'npm run build' and 'Search the dist/ output for any trace of react-grab. You should find none. Vite's dead-code elimination guarantees the dev-only import is stripped.'
